'''Whetu Taewa''' (born 19 October 1970) is a New Zealand former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who represented [[New Zealand national rugby league team|New Zealand]] six times between 1989 and 1996.<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/>

==Playing career==
===New Zealand===
Whetu was born on the [[West Coast, New Zealand]] and started playing [[rugby league]] there; all grades up to the [[West Coast rugby league team|West Coast Representative side]]. In 1987 he made the New Zealand under 17's training squad and also débuted in the [[West Coast Rugby Union|West Coast rugby union side]].<ref>''1987 Lion Red Rugby League Annual'', [[New Zealand Rugby Football League]], 1987. p.p.118-127</ref> Taewa made the [[Junior Kiwis]] in 1988. In 1989 he moved to [[Christchurch]] and played for the Hallswell club. This was the year he first made the [[New Zealand national rugby league team]], joining their tour to [[Great Britain]]. From 1990 to 1993 he was part of the successful [[Canterbury Rugby League|Canterbury]] side of the era and again joined the Kiwis on the [[1993 New Zealand rugby league tour of Great Britain and France|1993 tour of Great Britain and France]].<ref>[http://www.nzleague.co.nz/Players/kiwis_players_Selection.php?id=T&number=616 Kiwi Player Profiles] ''NZLeague.co.nz''</ref><ref name="Stuff.co.nz_2458687">{{cite news|url=http://www.stuff.co.nz/the-press/sport/2458687/Nothing-better-than-bettering-Auckland|title=Nothing better than bettering Auckland|author=Smith, Tony|date=30 May 2009|work=[[The Press]]|access-date=3 December 2011}}</ref> He played 4 games for the West Coast and 37 games for Canterbury.<ref>Richard Becht. ''A New Breed Rising: The Warriors Winfield Cup Challenge''. Auckland, HarperCollins, 1994. {{ISBN|1-86950-154-3}}. p.183</ref> He played in the [[1992 Pacific Cup (rugby league)|1992 Pacific Cup]] for the [[New Zealand Māori rugby league team|New Zealand Māori]] side. In 1993 he was invited to be part of an Auckland Invitational XIII side that drew 16-all with the [[Balmain Tigers]].<ref>Coffey, John and Bernie Wood ''Auckland, 100 years of rugby league, 1909-2009'', 2009. {{ISBN|978-1-86969-366-4}}, p.298.</ref>

In 1996 he played in the [[GB lions]] tour of [[New Zealand]] playing for the [[New Zealand Māori rugby league team|New Zealand Maori]] at centre coming up with a brilliant 40 points to 28 victory. 
It was the Māoris best win in over 15 years.

===Australian Rugby League===
In 1994 he signed with the [[Auckland Warriors]] who were to be a new team in the [[Australian Rugby League]] premiership. He moved up to [[Auckland]] and played for the [[Counties Manukau rugby league team#1994-1996: Heroes|Counties Manukau Heroes]] in the [[Lion Red Cup]] that year and represented [[Auckland rugby league team|Auckland]] in their Rugby League Cup challenge.<ref name=Auckland>''Lion Red Rugby League Annual 1994'', [[New Zealand Rugby Football League]], 1994. p.164</ref> In 1995 he was in the inaugural run on side for the Warriors in their first match against the [[Brisbane Broncos]].<ref>[http://www.warriors.co.nz/page/warriors_1995_2008_player_roster.html Warriors 1995-2008] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20080423201232/http://www.warriors.co.nz/page/warriors_1995_2008_player_roster.html |date=23 April 2008 }} ''Warriors Official Site''</ref> 
However, in 1996 he was released from the club and he joined the [[North Queensland Cowboys]].<ref>[http://stats.rleague.com/rl/scorers/players/Whetu_Taewa.html Try Scoreres: Whetu Taewa] ''rleague.com''</ref> Taewa toured PNG in 1996 with the [[New Zealand Māori rugby league team|New Zealand Māori]].<ref name=maori>{{cite book
| last =John Coffey, Bernie Wood
| title = 100 years: Māori rugby league, 1908-2008
| publisher =Huia Publishers
| year = 2008
| pages =  292
| url = https://books.google.com/books?id=nklWo8vw-iIC
| isbn =9781869693312}}</ref>

===England===
In 1997 he joined the [[Sheffield Eagles]] in the [[Super League]] competition.<ref>[http://www.rugby-league-world.com/1997/Results/StonesSL/Sheffield.html 1997 Super League: Sheffield Eagles] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160303181321/http://www.rugby-league-world.com/1997/Results/StonesSL/Sheffield.html |date=3 March 2016 }} ''rugby-league-world.com''</ref> Taewa played {{rlp|ce}} in Sheffield Eagles' 17–8 victory over [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] in the [[1998 Challenge Cup]] Final during [[Super League III]] at [[Wembley Stadium (1923)|Wembley Stadium]], [[London]] on Saturday 2 May 1998.

In 1999 he joined [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] who played in the [[Northern Ford Premiership]], the division below the Super League.<ref>[https://www.telegraph.co.uk/sport/main.jhtml?xml=/sport/2000/11/29/slgide30.xml 2000 Northern Ford Premiership Guide]{{dead link|date=July 2021|bot=medic}}{{cbignore|bot=medic}} ''The Telegraph'', 29 November 2000</ref> He became the captain in 2000, before retiring at the end of the 2002 season.

==Coaching and later years==
In 2003 he was an assistant coach for [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] before he returned home to New Zealand.<ref>[http://www.accessmylibrary.com/coms2/summary_0286-26558885_ITM Whetu Is Number Two As Linnane Makes Plans] ''Hull Daily Mail'', 28 October 2002</ref> Taewa left Hull KR after a family friend fell ill in New Zealand.<ref>[http://www.accessmylibrary.com/coms2/summary_0286-22307106_ITM 'It'S The Hardest Decision I'Ve Ever Made'] ''Hull Daily Mail'', 6 February 2003</ref> He now resides in [[Cromwell, New Zealand|Cromwell]].<ref name="TVNZ_592679">{{cite news|url=http://tvnz.co.nz/view/page/411317/592679|title=Warriors set to turn back time|date=17 June 2005|work=[[One Sport]]|access-date=3 December 2011}}</ref>

==Honours==
As Taewa represented the [[New Zealand national rugby league team]] while playing for the Haswell club, he was rewarded with Honorary life membership at the club.<ref>[http://www.hornets.co.nz/the-club/kiwi-honours-board.aspx Kiwis Honours Board] ''Hornets.co.nz''</ref>
